title: BEAM Therapeutics BEACON Trial Risto-cel Data Publication
text: Beam Therapeutics announced publication of BEACON Phase 1/2 trial data for ristoglogene autogetemcel (risto-cel) in patients with sickle cell disease in The New England Journal of Medicine. Risto-cel is a one-time, ex vivo base editing cell therapy consisting of autologous CD34+ hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells base-edited in promoter regions of HBG1/2 genes. The therapy is designed to inhibit transcriptional repressor BCL11A from binding without disrupting BCL11A expression, leading to increased production of non-sickling and anti-sickling fetal hemoglobin. Patients achieved mean Hemoglobin F levels above 60% maintained for up to 20 months with Hemoglobin S levels consistently reduced below 40%. All treated patients experienced resolution of anemia after elimination of transfused blood. The company expects to submit a Biologics License Application for risto-cel as early as year-end 2026 for sickle cell disease treatment.
